title: SliderSandbox props
description: Interactive sandbox with 1-6 sliders wired to a live equation, optional SVG, and formatted readouts. The moat widget — nothing else on the market auto-generates working physics simulations from lecture content.

title.description: Short heading shown above the controls

key.description: JS identifier used as `vars.<key>` in compute

initial.description: Starting value. Must match kind (number for range/number, string for select)

options.description: Required when kind=select. Array of {value, label} or plain strings.

outputs.description: Named readouts computed from the variables

key.description: Matches a key in the compute() return object

unit.description: Unit suffix. Use SI form; the widget picks the prefix.

compute.description: JS function body. Receives { vars, consts, status } and returns { <output.key>: number, status?: string, svg?: string }. Example: 'const F = 0.5*consts.eps0*vars.A*vars.V*vars.V/(vars.x*vars.x); return { F, vpi: Math.sqrt(8*vars.k*Math.pow(vars.x,3)/(27*consts.eps0*vars.A)) };'

constants.description: Named physical constants accessible as `consts.<key>` in compute. Example: { eps0: 8.854e-12, mu0: 1.2566e-6 }

svgTemplate.description: Optional. When present, compute() MAY override via its svg return value; otherwise this is used as-is.

description.description: Static explanation rendered below the readouts

tip.description: Optional 'try this' nudge shown in small print. Encourages intuition-building.
